payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Weekly aggregation of earned amounts for children
Parent reporting interface aggregates child earnings and task completion data

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming by child
payload.justification: SG049 identifies claiming as a strategic process that children perform to take ownership of tasks.

payload.description: Task completion tracking
payload.justification: SG049 establishes tracking of completed tasks as a fundamental process in the pocket money system.

payload.description: Task assignment definition by parent
payload.justification: SG050 describes the parental process of defining compensated task assignments with monetary values.

payload.description: Compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 identifies distribution of compensation as the strategic outcome process enabled by performance reporting.

payload.description: Task assignment (for pocket money)
payload.justification: Signals SG049 and SG051 explicitly reference tasks as the fundamental unit of work that children complete for compensation. This is a core strategic entity in the pocket money domain. | SG050 describes parental definition of compensated task assignments, establishing assignments as a distinct entity linking tasks to monetary value and children.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG052 identifies the earnings summary as a strategically important aggregated view of child compensation over a weekly period.

payload.description: Child performance report
payload.justification: SG053 establishes performance reporting as a distinct entity supporting parental compensation decisions, separate from raw task data.

payload.description: Task history
payload.justification: SG054 identifies longitudinal task history as a strategically important record supporting accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: SG050 explicitly mentions associated monetary values as a defining characteristic of compensated task assignments.

payload.description: Weekly task and earnings cycle
payload.justification: Both SG049 and SG052 explicitly reference weekly periodicity for task tracking and earnings summary, establishing this as a strategic temporal cycle.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Multiple signals (SG049, SG051, SG052) identify children as a primary strategic role with specific capabilities: claiming tasks, viewing inventory, and accessing earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: SG050 and SG053 establish parents as a distinct strategic role with responsibilities for defining assignments and accessing performance reports.

payload.description: Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: SG049 explicitly expresses this as the fundamental purpose of the system from the child perspective.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation to create and manage task assignments with monetary values.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into task inventory
payload.justification: SG051 captures the purpose of enabling children to see both available and completed tasks.

payload.description: Enable children to view weekly earnings
payload.justification: SG052 identifies the motivational goal of providing earnings visibility to children.

payload.description: Support accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 establishes the purpose of performance reporting as enabling accurate payment to children.

payload.description: Support accountability and trend analysis through task history
payload.justification: SG054 identifies the strategic requirement for longitudinal history access supporting accountability and analytical purposes.

payload.description: Specify task with monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 defines parent capability to create task entities with monetary attributes

payload.description: Claim completed task
payload.justification: SG290 establishes claiming as core business process; SG292 specifies linkage mechanism

payload.description: View available tasks
payload.justification: SG293 defines visibility requirements for available task entities

payload.description: View completed tasks
payload.justification: SG293 defines visibility requirements for completed task entities

payload.description: Aggregate weekly earnings
payload.justification: SG294 establishes weekly aggregation capability for monetary totals

payload.description: Generate parent report of child earnings
payload.justification: SG295 defines parent reporting capability to display child-task-earnings relationships

payload.description: Task availability resets weekly
payload.justification: SG296 specifies cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly boundaries

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention and accessibility as business rules for historical task data

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines task entities with monetary value attributes; SG290 establishes tasks as core business concept | SG292 distinguishes completed task entities; SG290 references completed tasks in core business concept

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: SG291 specifies associated monetary value as an attribute of task entities

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: SG292 defines child entities; SG293 establishes child actors as business entities with visibility requirements

payload.description: Child claims completed task
payload.justification: SG290 establishes claiming as core relationship; SG292 specifies linkage between child and completed task entities

payload.description: Weekly earnings total for child
payload.justification: SG294 defines aggregated monetary totals as a business entity with weekly scope

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association for reporting
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly defines child-task-earnings relationships as reportable business data

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention of historical task data entities across temporal periods

payload.description: Weekly period for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG294 defines weekly aggregation cycle; SG296 specifies weekly temporal boundaries for task reset

payload.description: Task completion
payload.justification: SG290 and SG292 reference completed tasks as a state change event triggering claim eligibility

payload.description: Weekly boundary triggers task availability reset
payload.justification: SG296 specifies weekly temporal boundaries as trigger for task availability reset

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: SG291 defines parent capability to specify tasks; SG295 defines parent reporting capability

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: SG290 establishes children as core actors claiming tasks; SG293 defines child visibility requirements; SG294 specifies child as recipient of earnings visibility

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks
payload.justification: SG290 establishes monetary reward tracking as core business purpose

payload.description: Support payment fulfillment to children
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly states payment fulfillment as purpose for parent reporting capability

payload.description: Historical task data must be accessible across time periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention and accessibility as business constraint on historical data

payload.description: Task completion events trigger association with child entities
payload.justification: SG457 describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, clearly a business rule governing process behavior.

payload.description: Child users have constrained access patterns to task collections based on visibility requirements
payload.justification: SG458 specifies data visibility requirements which translate to logical access control rules governing how child users interact with task collections.

payload.description: Parent reporting interface aggregates child earnings and task completion data on a weekly basis
payload.justification: SG459 describes temporal aggregation logic as a logical function that computes and presents weekly earned amounts. | SG460 describes a logical reporting interface as a function that aggregates and presents data to parents.

payload.description: Periodic state reset process restores task availability on weekly cycle
payload.justification: SG461 describes periodic state reset logic as a logical process that restores task availability.

payload.description: Child entity is associated with task completion event
payload.justification: SG457 implies a logical relationship between child entities and task completion events at the data model level.

payload.description: Task collection logical entity
payload.justification: SG458 references task collections as a logical data entity subject to visibility requirements.

payload.description: Earned amount attribute of child entity
payload.justification: SG459 references earned amounts as a logical attribute associated with children that requires weekly aggregation.

payload.description: Child earnings logical entity
payload.justification: SG460 references child earnings as a logical data entity included in parent reporting.

payload.description: Task completion data logical entity
payload.justification: SG460 references task completion data as a logical entity aggregated for parent reporting.

payload.description: Task availability state attribute
payload.justification: SG461 implies a task availability state as a logical attribute that undergoes periodic reset.

payload.description: Historical task record logical entity
payload.justification: SG462 references historical task records as a logical data entity subject to retention requirements.

payload.description: Weekly computation cycle for child earnings presentation
payload.justification: SG459 defines a weekly temporal cycle governing when earned amounts are computed and presented.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing task availability restoration
payload.justification: SG461 explicitly defines a weekly cycle as the temporal pattern for task availability reset behavior.

payload.description: Parent role with access to aggregated child data reporting
payload.justification: SG460 identifies parents as a logical role that consumes aggregated reporting functionality.

payload.description: Child user role
payload.justification: SG458 references child users as a logical role with specific access patterns to task collections.

payload.description: Historical task records must be logically persistent and accessible
payload.justification: SG462 mandates data retention as a design constraint requiring persistence and accessibility of historical records.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions at the physical/technical level, aligning with Why column technical constraints.

payload.description: Weekly earnings calculation cycle boundary for child access to earned totals
payload.justification: Signal SG542 establishes a weekly operational schedule boundary governing when earned amounts become accessible, representing a detailed timing cycle specification.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle for claiming operations
payload.justification: Signal SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset operations, representing a distinct temporal cycle from the earnings cycle.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 identifies iOS as a named deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target. | Signal SG545 identifies Android as a named deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 identifies Windows as a named deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target.

payload.description: Child user executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signal SG539 explicitly identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming activities. This maps directly to the Actor classification within the Who column at the Implementer level.

payload.description: Parent user performing task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: Signal SG540 explicitly identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task setup and monetary assignment. This is a distinct Actor from children, requiring separate CCI per Rule 5.

payload.description: Child role interface for viewing available and completed task records
payload.justification: Signal SG541 defines operational visibility requirements specific to child actors, representing a detailed interface role specification at the Implementer level.

payload.description: Parent role interface for accessing pocket money distribution reports
payload.justification: Signal SG543 defines parent operational role in report access and distribution activities, representing a detailed role interface specification distinct from the parent actor CCI.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
